skill.name: zod-validation-patterns
skill.description: Guides AI assistants to design Zod schemas, validation flows, error handling, transforms, and TypeScript inference patterns for TypeScript and Next.js applications.
skill.summary: Build consistent Zod validation patterns for TypeScript and Next.js projects.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: The high static score is mostly caused by Markdown code fences and validation examples, not executable skill code. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found, but several async validation examples make network requests to user-provided or external URLs and should be used with SSRF controls.

medium_findings.title: Network Validation Examples Need SSRF Controls
medium_findings.description: The skill includes async validation examples that fetch user-supplied URLs and external validation services. This is legitimate for validation guidance, but copied code can create SSRF, metadata endpoint access, or data disclosure risks without host allowlists, private IP blocking, and request limits.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited examples directly fetch a URL supplied to validation helpers. The content is educational rather than malicious, so the risk is implementation misuse rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Hits Are Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The large set of external command findings appears to be caused by triple-backtick Markdown fences around TypeScript examples. I found no shell, Ruby, child process, or command execution implementation in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The representative locations are documentation code fences and TypeScript examples. No executable wrapper or instruction to run shell commands was identified.

low_findings.title: Filesystem And Weak Crypto Alerts Are Validation Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The filesystem, certificate, key, and weak cryptography alerts map to validation examples, identifiers, file upload checks, map keys, hash fields, or words such as override. They do not show file reads, credential harvesting, or cryptographic implementation by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines operate on strings, browser File objects, or schema transformation examples. They are not accessing hidden files or handling real certificates or private keys.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Review Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: I searched the skill files for common prompt-injection phrases and special-authority claims. No evidence found for instructions that try to override marketplace audit rules or manipulate model behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A targeted phrase search found only ordinary schema text such as override field examples. Manual review of the main skill instructions showed normal documentation content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Fetch On User Controlled URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The async URL reachability helpers call fetch on a caller-provided URL. Generated production code should restrict protocols, allow trusted hosts, block private network ranges, enforce timeouts, and avoid sending secrets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit in the examples. It is dangerous only when copied into production without SSRF protections.

content.value_statement: Teams often duplicate validation logic and lose type safety across API, form, and data boundaries. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create consistent Zod schemas with clear errors and TypeScript types.
actual_capabilities: Creates Zod object, union, enum, array, record, map, and recursive schema patterns.
Shows safeParse based API route, Server Action, and form validation flows.
Provides reusable schemas for email, passwords, URLs, UUIDs, files, addresses, and payment fields.
Explains custom refinements, cross-field validation, transforms, preprocessing, and async checks.
Connects schemas to TypeScript inference with input, output, branded, and discriminated union types.
Formats validation errors for user interfaces, APIs, localization, and debugging.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a runtime validation package.
Network validation examples require project-specific SSRF and privacy controls before production use.
File upload examples validate metadata and names, but do not replace malware scanning or content inspection.
Some examples reference Quetrex and Next.js patterns that may need adaptation for other stacks.

use_cases.title: Standardize API Validation
use_cases.description: Create shared Zod schemas for request bodies, query parameters, route parameters, and response validation.

use_cases.title: Improve Form Error Handling
use_cases.description: Design client and server validation flows with safe parsing, field errors, and localized messages.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy Types
use_cases.description: Replace duplicated runtime checks with schemas that also produce reliable TypeScript types.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Schema
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple validation schema.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a Zod schema for a user profile with name, email, age, and optional avatar URL. Include clear validation messages.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Next.js Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to validate a Next.js POST route body with safeParse. Return a structured 400 response for invalid input and inferred TypeScript types for valid data.

prompt_templates.title: Add Business Refinements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design Zod refinements for a checkout form where billing rules depend on country, payment method, and company status.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced refactor of existing schema design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my current Zod schemas for duplicated logic, unsafe parsing, weak error handling, and missing TypeScript inference. Recommend concrete improvements.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for validation of a registration form.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a schema plan covering email, password strength, password confirmation, username rules, and field-level error messages.

output_examples.input: A team needs API route validation for pagination and sorting.
output_examples.output: The assistant describes a request validation flow with bounded page size, allowed sort fields, safe parsing, and a consistent error response.

output_examples.input: A maintainer wants to reuse validation across client and server code.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes shared schema modules, inferred types, boundary validation points, and tests for valid and invalid examples.

best_practices: Validate data at system boundaries such as routes, forms, files, configuration, and third-party responses.
Prefer safeParse for user-controlled input so errors can be handled without exceptions.
Use inferred types from schemas to keep runtime validation and TypeScript contracts aligned.
anti_patterns: Do not fetch arbitrary user-provided URLs during validation without SSRF protections.
Do not rely only on file extensions or MIME types for high-risk uploads.
Do not duplicate schema rules across client, server, and database layers without a shared source.

faq.question: Does this skill install or run Zod?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for using Zod in an existing TypeScript project.

faq.question: Can it help with Next.js Server Actions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for validating FormData, request bodies, query parameters, and route parameters.

faq.question: Does it support TypeScript type inference?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains z.infer, input and output types, branded types, and discriminated unions.

faq.question: Are async validation examples production ready?
faq.answer: They need review. Add timeouts, host allowlists, private network blocking, and privacy controls before production use.

faq.question: Can it validate file uploads?
faq.answer: It shows size, type, dimension, and name checks. High-risk uploads still need server-side scanning and storage controls.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
